name: karen-jack-test
description: Karen loses her job and does what she does best—complains. A lot. Turns out society was just waiting for someone angry enough to never shut up.

config.text: Office building in New York, Karen’s the head of the HR department where she works. Basically gets paid to harass people.
Office building in New York, Karen’s the head of the HR department where she works. Basically gets paid to harass people.

text_bubbles.text: I’ve fired 8 people this week for micro-aggressions, little man, do you want to be next?

config.text: Karen is showing off her usual charm.

text_bubbles.text: Now, shoe boy, I’ve got important work to do, unlike some of us.

config.text: Karen gets her phone out, ignoring her surroundings.

text_bubbles.text: Karen microwaving fish... again, kill me please, Lord.

config.text: Karen gets her phone out, ignoring her surroundings.

text_bubbles.text: Make sure you get that spot at the back, do you need me to tell you how to do your oh so complex job, hun?

config.text: Karen gets her phone out, ignoring her surroundings.

text_bubbles.text: I'm sorry Karen, I have to let you go, AI Shaun has deemed the entire HR Department completly useless, not to kick you while you are down or anything.
The fucking gall...
I'm sorry Karen, I have to let you go, AI Shaun has deemed the entire HR Department completly useless, not to kick you while you are down or anything.
The fucking gall...
So you got replaced by AI. What does a robot know about people?
The injustice of this heinous crime should be shouted from the rooftops, being victimised by the oppressive…
Get out of the way, that’s right, pedal away, pumpkin!

config.text: They walk around ranting for two hours straight.

text_bubbles.text: The gall...

config.text: They wanted to have one drink, just to take the edge off...

text_bubbles.text: What are they gonna do without me?
Who will whip that place into shape? Not fucking limp-dick Brian, that's for sure!

config.text: Moving swiftly on to cocktails, mixed with their collective hatred for humanity, it adds flavour.

text_bubbles.text: I'm not drunk, you're drunk!
Need a tissue, I got ideas, hun...

config.text: They all of a sudden became completely fine after a bathroom visit. Weird.

text_bubbles.text: And another thing!
The fuck was that shi...
Trust me I got plan, but that can wait I need a few sleeping pills another glass of the good stuff.
Why don’t you do what you always do when justice needs to prevail? Call the manager.

config.text: The next day. Finally, Lauren pawns her off, making something up to lose her, like throwing a ball for a dog.

text_bubbles.text: Please, hold.
Yeah, I’ll wait, buddy, I’ve got all day to complain.

config.text: Karen spends all afternoon on the phone with local politicians, who keep putting her on hold.

text_bubbles.text: Please, hold.
Please, hold.

config.text: Karen is draining the life out of each receptionist, local politician, and even the automated voice saying, “Please hold.”

text_bubbles.text: Sir i am an american and I have rights…

config.text: She persists all day and night, in the last phone booth in New York, you have a mobile that has more computing power than the system that got us to the moon, Karen.
Now most people would give up here, but Karen, she ain’t most people.
Now most people would give up here, but Karen, she ain’t most people.

text_bubbles.text: All day to complain.

config.text: As the sun sets… again, in multiple phone booths. A music producer, Aaron, walks past, a miracle he hasn’t been robbed and shot yet.
He hears Karen say, “All day to complain,” and something about it sounded good, so he samples it and makes a beat on the spot.
Karen Going viral on Soundcloud, who the fuck would’a thought it?

text_bubbles.text: You heard that Karen song then?
What like the meme?
Shut up and listen.
We should go to where they are live streaming this!
Yeah this is a banger.

config.text: A crowd forms, so many people live-stream Karen. Being completely oblivious to all the noise around her, she complains into the ether.
​A typical frantic night behind the scenes: The Late Night Talk Show with Ted Connors.

text_bubbles.text: You heard of this karen thing, she wants to talk to the fuckin’ president or some shit, we’ve got him on tonight… maybe-
Maybe- we have the guy talk to the crazy bastard in the phone booth live on air, you heard the song? not bad let me tell you.
Alright we would get decent views from this, or at least a clip on social media. Get on the phone!
Finally someone with some sense, yes young man, I will hold…
Is this Karen, hey I’m James, producer of late night, we have the president on tonight, would you stay there and talk to him when we call again?

config.text: Karen’s entitled response baffles the producer.

text_bubbles.text: And they said hip hop died with Biggie…
Ladies and gentlemen…

config.text: The president of the United States goes on a talk show to try to get votes for the upcoming election. Shameful.

text_bubbles.text: Karen, the viral sensation, has a few words she'd like to share with you. Are you happy to speak with her, Mr President?
Of course, Ted.

config.text: Talk show hosts have in their contract where they have to lick their guest’s arse, especially when it’s a politician.

text_bubbles.text: Finally…

config.text: They get Karen on the line, she has been waiting there solidly for days.

text_bubbles.text: Hold on, Karen!

config.text: The president gets a text that rattles him…
The president gets a text that rattles him…

text_bubbles.text: Karen, we may have a solution for this AI issue…

config.text: The president looks sincerely down the camera and attempts to say something honest, for the first time in his life.

text_bubbles.text: Once AI took jobs from the middle class, that's where things changed…

config.text: The year is 2125, in a University building in New York. This place has changed a tad. Professor Davies opens his lecture on the history of the Karen Movement.
The year is 2125, in a University building in New York. This place has changed a tad. Professor Davies opens his lecture on the history of the Karen Movement.
The year is 2125, in a University building in New York. This place has changed a tad. Professor Davies opens his lecture on the history of the Karen Movement.
The year is 2125, in a University building in New York. This place has changed a tad. Professor Davies opens his lecture on the history of the Karen Movement.
